content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN Integers which are initially all 000. You are also given an array BBB consisting of NNN Integers. You have to tell whether you can make AAA equal to BBB or not by performing the following operation any number of times.

In one operation you can choose exactly KKK distinct indices in the array A and increase the value at these indices by 111.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains two lines of input.
First line contains two Integers NNN and KKK.
Second line contains NNN Integers denoting array BBB.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line YES if AAA can be converted to BBB, otherwise print NO.

###Constraints


1≤T≤10001 \leq T \leq 10001≤T≤1000
1≤N≤1051 \leq N \leq 10^51≤N≤105
1≤K≤N1 \leq K \leq N1≤K≤N
1≤B[i]≤1091 \leq B[i] \leq 10^91≤B[i]≤109
Sum of N over all Testcases does not exceed 10610^6106.
